statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the second largest element in the array. If no such element exists, return -1. The input array may contain duplicate elements.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the array
The ith element in the array

examples.explanation: The second largest element in the array is 9.
There is no second largest element in the array.
The second largest element in the array is 10.
The second largest element in the array is 30.

hints.text: To find the second largest element, you should first consider how to find the largest element in the array, and then think about how to distinguish the second largest from other elements.

A straightforward approach could involve sorting the array, but this might not be the most efficient way, especially for large arrays. Consider if you can solve it with a single pass through the array.

Think about initializing two variables to keep track of the maximum and second maximum values seen so far. Update these variables as you iterate through the array, considering the case where the array contains duplicate elements.

editorial.title: Finding the Second Largest Element in an Array
editorial.intuition: The problem requires finding the second largest distinct element in an array. A brute force approach would involve sorting the array and then picking the second largest element, but this is inefficient for large arrays.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force solution would involve sorting the array first, which takes O(n log n) time, and then selecting the second largest element. This approach is correct but slow for large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a set to store distinct elements and then sort the set to find the second largest element.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a set to store distinct elements in O(n) time and then sorts the set in O(n log n) time. However, we can do better than this by finding the maximum and second maximum in a single pass through the array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proved by observing that the set of distinct elements contains all unique elements from the array. Sorting this set and selecting the second last element gives the second largest distinct element. The known time complexity of this approach is O(n) time and O(n) space.
editorial.dryRun: For example, given the array [4, 2, 9, 6, 5], the set of distinct elements is {4, 2, 9, 6, 5}. Sorting this set gives [2, 4, 5, 6, 9], so the second largest element is 8 is incorrect since 8 is not in the array; the correct answer is 6.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case when there are less than two distinct elements in the array.
Using a single pass through the array but not correctly updating the maximum and second maximum.
Not considering the case when the array contains duplicate elements.
Incorrectly assuming that the second largest element is the second element in the sorted array.
Not checking for the case when the input array is empty.
